element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-implicit; players immediately face weakest enemies like Bullet
Durable enough to teach combo timing but low threat
Environmental destruction signals progression; fence breach signals warp point
Character signature move; more effective than basic attacks
Character signature move; powerful but slower execution
Optional resource; extends combo chains and damage potential
Risk/reward mechanic: grabbing leaves character vulnerable to hits (Japanese version) or safer (US version)
Temporary power-up triggered by damage; risk-reward for aggressive play

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of Stage One and subsequent stages
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Pattern-based encounters requiring learned attack timing
Conditional skip system: requires exact kill count or precise positioning; rewards exploration
Culmination of learned mechanics; Japanese version reveals boss is character's father

typical_tension_triggers: Grabbing enemies (vulnerability window in Japanese version)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation across stages with boss punctuation; tension peaks at multi-enemy encounters and boss fights; relief through weapon pickups and special moves; overall progression feels linear with minimal downtime

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Warp point locations marked by environmental features (fence breach, doorways, stairways); requires specific positioning or kill count conditions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Press Up at specific locations to activate warps; four warp points exist (two normally accessible); warp room mini-challenge gates stage skips

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with weak enemies (Bullet class); basic attack patterns only; builds familiarity with punch/kick/jump mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy variety and density; introduction of stronger enemy types; warp point conditions add skill-gating; special moves become necessary
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high enemy density; multi-stage boss patterns; final confrontation with father reveals narrative twist in Japanese version
notable_spikes: Stage One boss transition from minions to named encounter
Stages requiring exactly 30 or 66 kills for warp activation (skill/persistence gates)
Final stage convergence toward Big Al/father reveal

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage divides into two areas with distinct enemy configurations; area transitions occur between minor and major encounters; warp points placed at area transitions to reward skilled players
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage One: Area One (initial enemies) → Area Two (warp eligible); mirrored in Stages 4-6

notable_level_patterns.description: Major named bosses (Big Al, Street Kings lieutenants) terminate stage progression; requires pattern learning and special move application; serves as difficulty wall before next stage
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage conclusion encounters with Kato, Arnold Gigante, Captain Sledge, etc.

notable_level_patterns.description: Warp points locked behind precise requirements (kill counts 4, 30, 66) or positioning puzzles; allows sequence breaking for speedrun/skilled play but remains optional
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stages Four, Five, Six warp point conditions

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Rival Turf! article)
